mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue beats that lift S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing equity risk premiums and pushing S&P 500 forward returns higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surging above its 12-month moving average signals supply-side capacity expansion that outpaces demand, compressing profit margins and depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal exhibits no predictive power when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand that directly supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in over 50% of observations where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the rank correlation between GDPC1 surprises and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation accelerating past 0.3 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, elevating real interest rates and compressing equity multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3 percent threshold, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand shock that boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate a positive risk-adjusted return spread of at least 100 basis points over a 1-year rolling window, or the interaction term becomes insignificant when controlling for INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real personal savings exceeding 5 percent signals household balance sheet stress that curtails discretionary spending, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered episodes, or the signal exhibits no predictive power when controlling for concurrent GDPC1 growth.
